notes: Top 10 GDPR obligations as auditable data checks. v0.1.0 - proof of concept. Not legal advice.

obligations.title: Lawfulness - Consent Field Present
obligations.description: Personal data must be processed lawfully. Consent basis must be recorded.

obligations.title: Purpose Limitation
obligations.description: Data collected for specified purposes must not be processed for incompatible purposes.

obligations.title: Data Minimisation
obligations.description: Only data adequate and relevant to the purpose should be collected.
obligations.notes: Automated check flags records with unusually high field density vs. declared purpose.

obligations.title: Accuracy - Required Fields Not Null
obligations.description: Personal data must be accurate and kept up to date.

obligations.title: Storage Limitation - Retention Timestamp Present
obligations.description: Data must not be kept longer than necessary.

obligations.title: Transparency - Privacy Notice Reference
obligations.description: Data subjects must be informed about data processing at collection time.

obligations.title: Right to Erasure - Deletion Flag Present
obligations.description: Mechanism must exist to honor erasure requests.

obligations.title: Data Portability - Export Mechanism
obligations.description: Data subjects have the right to receive their data in a structured, machine-readable format.
obligations.notes: Flags datasets with no documented export pathway.

obligations.title: Data Protection by Design - Pseudonymisation Field
obligations.description: Data protection must be built into processing systems by design.

obligations.title: Breach Notification - Incident Log Present
obligations.description: Breaches affecting personal data must be reported to supervisory authority within 72 hours.
obligations.notes: Rell flags any incident record where the gap between detected_at and notified_at exceeds 72 hours.
